title: Hoffmannia gesnerioides
intro: Hoffmannia gesnerioides is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, commonly known as "Coyotillo" or "Matacuncho", which is a type of climbing vine belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hoffmannia gesnerioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Hoffmannia gesnerioides is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family includes around 600 genera and over 13,000 species, many of which are tropical and subtropical in distribution. Hoffmannia gesnerioides is native to Central America, particularly in countries such as Costa Rica, Panama, and Belize. Its classification is further divided into the tribe Psychotrieae, which is a group of plants characterized by their showy flowers and woody stems.
